High_Requirements: A Python interface shall be implemented
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a generic iterator, to enable the developer to iterate over all WARCrecords and create an abstract WARC-document as a simple container
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement a generic iterator in libwarc that iterates over all WARC records and builds an abstract WARC document as a simple container.
Emphasis & Caution: Must cover all WARC records.
Things to Avoid: Do not hard-code record types.

High_Requirements: An independent Java implementation of libwarc may be implemented
Low_Requirements: A set of command line tools incorporating libwarc shall perform migration of "HTTrack" archives to WARC-records.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, build command-line tools using libwarc to migrate HTTrack archives into WARC records.
Emphasis & Caution: Ensure correct archive-to-WARC mapping.
Things to Avoid: Do not alter original archive content semantics.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a default iterator as a built-in function
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a generic iterator, to enable the developer to iterate over all WARCrecords and create an abstract WARC-document as a simple container
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement a generic iterator in libwarc that iterates over all WARC records and creates an abstract WARC document as a simple container.
Emphasis & Caution: Must support full iteration without data loss.
Things to Avoid: Do not skip or filter records implicitly.

High_Requirements: Dynamic memory management shall be hidden inside libwarc
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall use dynamic heap memory for its internal usage.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement libwarc to use dynamic heap memory for all internal operations.
Emphasis & Caution: Manage memory safely and efficiently.
Things to Avoid: Do not rely on static or fixed-size memory.

High_Requirements: For each type of WARC-record, a set of functions shall be made available to create/modify the records properties.
Low_Requirements: Each peer class shall expose a set a class functions to read, write, and update attributes for the corresponding WARC-record.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement peer classes that expose class functions to read, write, and update attributes of corresponding WARC records.
Emphasis & Caution: Functions must fully reflect record attributes.
Things to Avoid: Do not provide read-only access only.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall enable combinations of iterators to be used, i.e. composite iterators
Low_Requirements: Libwarc's iterators may be combined into composite iterators to enable the developer to more than one search field
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, enable libwarc iterators to be combined into composite iterators supporting multiple search fields.
Emphasis & Caution: Combined iterators must work simultaneously.
Things to Avoid: Do not limit iteration to a single field.

High_Requirements: It shall be possible to convert ARC files to WARC files using a command-line tool called "arc2warc"
Low_Requirements: A set of command line tools incorporating libwarc shall perform migration of "curl" archives to WARC-records.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, build command-line tools using libwarc to migrate curl archives into WARC records.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve request and response data accurately.
Things to Avoid: Do not drop metadata during migration.

High_Requirements: It shall be possible to walk a wget mirror and write the data to WARC files
Low_Requirements: Python scripts shall be implemented incorporating libwarc, and making all of the functionality of libwarc and API available in Python.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement Python scripts that integrate libwarc and expose its full functionality and API to Python.
Emphasis & Caution: Ensure full feature parity with libwarc.
Things to Avoid: Do not expose partial or limited APIs.

High_Requirements: WARC extensions shall be released as a patch to each of the commands "curl", "wget", and "httrack" projects
Low_Requirements: A web proxy interface shall be implemented that allows the user to set their web browser proxy to the one provided by the interface and thereby ensure all content is delivered from the archive and not from the live web.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement a web proxy interface that serves archived content only when a browser is configured to use it.
Emphasis & Caution: All content must come from the archive, not the live web.
Things to Avoid: Do not allow fallback to live web content.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide an abstract interface for iterators, to enable custom iterators
Low_Requirements: Libwarc's generic iterators may be customised for different purposes via callback handlers (i.e. hooks)
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, enable customization of libwarc generic iterators using callback handlers.
Emphasis & Caution: Callbacks must be flexible and reusable.
Things to Avoid: Do not hard-code customization logic.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a range of functions through a universal interface for creating each type of valid WARC-record, thus ensuring the resulting WARC files conform to the prevailing WARC standard
Low_Requirements: Libwarc's iterators may be combined into composite iterators to enable the developer to more than one search field
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, enable libwarc iterators to be combined into composite iterators that support multiple search fields.
Emphasis & Caution: Ensure multiple fields can be queried simultaneously.
Things to Avoid: Do not restrict iteration to a single field.

High_Requirements: It shall be possible to walk a curl directory and write the data to WARC files
Low_Requirements: A set of command line tools incorporating libwarc shall perform migration of "curl" archives to WARC-records.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, create command-line tools using libwarc to migrate curl archives into WARC records.
Emphasis & Caution: Preserve archive content and metadata accurately.
Things to Avoid: Do not lose request or response data.

High_Requirements: ARC to WARC conversions made by arc2warc shall be carried out according to a specification in a configuration file
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide an API describing (1) the set of data, and (2) the set of operations that can be performed on the data. The data types shall be abstract (abstract data types - ADT), to ensure independence of concrete implementations.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, define an abstract API in libwarc that specifies data sets and supported operations using abstract data types.
Emphasis & Caution: API must be independent of concrete implementations.
Things to Avoid: Do not expose implementation-specific details.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide ARC-record reader functionality Libwarc reader will thereby make read operations as simple as possible.
Low_Requirements: Helper documentation for libwarc functionality shall be made available within the "HTTrack", "wget" and "curl" commands.

Definition: In this task, provide helper documentation for libwarc features within HTTrack, wget, and curl commands.
Emphasis & Caution: Documentation must be accessible from command help.
Things to Avoid: Do not place docs outside command interfaces.

High_Requirements: WARC extensions help content will be provided each of the commands "curl", "wget", and "httrack"
Low_Requirements: Helper documentation for libwarc functionality shall be made available within the "HTTrack", "wget" and "curl" commands.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, provide helper documentation for libwarc features within HTTrack, wget, and curl commands.
Emphasis & Caution: Documentation must be consistent across all commands.
Things to Avoid: Do not create command-specific discrepancies.

High_Requirements: The libwarc interfaces shall fully encapsulate and internal functionality, isolating by abstraction any tool or application based on libwarc from changes in the internal implementation.
Low_Requirements: The universal header "warc.h" shall ensure compatibility between all versions of libwarc.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, ensure the universal header warc.h maintains compatibility across all libwarc versions.
Emphasis & Caution: Backward compatibility must be preserved.
Things to Avoid: Do not introduce version-breaking changes.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a stable Application Programme Interface (API) to handle WARCrecords as abstract objects (ADT)
Low_Requirements: Any operations on WARC-records shall be possible using functions accepting the abstract handle as an argument.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, enable all WARC record operations through functions that accept an abstract handle.
Emphasis & Caution: All operations must use the abstract handle.
Things to Avoid: Do not access records directly.

High_Requirements: Dynamic memory management shall be hidden inside libwarc
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall not use file to memory mapping technology, instead libwarc will explicitly allocate memory as needed.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement libwarc to allocate memory explicitly instead of using file-to-memory mapping.
Emphasis & Caution: Manage memory allocation safely.

High_Requirements: Dynamic memory management shall be hidden inside libwarc

Emphasis & Caution: All public access must go through warc.h.

High_Requirements: A Jhove plugin module and an output handler shall be made available for WARC files
Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall support multiple compression schemas, loading a specific compressor at runtime as an external shared library.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, support multiple compression schemes in libwarc by loading compressors at runtime as shared libraries.
Emphasis & Caution: Compression modules must be dynamically loadable.
Things to Avoid: Do not hard-code compression implementations.

High_Requirements: Libwarc shall be memory safe and manage dynamic memory internally
Low_Requirements: WARC files in various test-states shall be provided that test the Jhove deliverables
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, provide WARC files in multiple test states to validate Jhove deliverables.
Emphasis & Caution: Test states must cover valid and invalid cases.
Things to Avoid: Do not provide only fully valid files.

High_Requirements: The C library shall be implemented to be compatible with the Simplified Wrapper and Interface Generator (SWIG, http://www.swig.org) - a wrapper for libwarc C code to allow it to be called natively in a wide variety of languages.
Low_Requirements: Libwarc's generic iterators may be customised for different purposes via callback handlers (i.e. hooks)
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, enable customization of libwarc generic iterators using callback handlers.
Emphasis & Caution: Callbacks must alter iterator behavior.

High_Requirements: The functionality of the library shall be exposed in such a way as to fit the metaphors and paradigms of the implementation language
Low_Requirements: The universal header "warc.h" shall ensure compatibility between all versions of libwarc.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, ensure the universal header warc.h maintains compatibility across all libwarc versions.

Definition: In this task, implement a command-line tool using libwarc to validate WARC record consistency and ISO compliance.

High_Requirements: It shall be possible to convert ARC files to WARC files using a command-line tool called "arc2warc"

Low_Requirements: Libwarc shall provide a WARC-record MIME-type iterator
Instruction:

High_Requirements: For each type of WARC-record, a set of functions shall be made available to create/modify the records properties.

Low_Requirements: A set of command line tools and an API incorporating libwarc shall enable the collection of online documents, such as html and embedded files, etc., and write them to valid WARC- records.

High_Requirements: A Python script shall be made available to enable rapid development of WARC based solutions
Low_Requirements: Python scripts shall be implemented incorporating libwarc, and making all of the functionality of libwarc and API available in Python.
Instruction:
Definition: In this task, implement Python scripts that expose the full libwarc functionality and API in Python.
